content: Hi, I'm Cameron White, a Marketing Manager at a SaaS company. I'm expecting my first child in a few months and I'm thinking about making a career change. I've always been interested in stand-up comedy and I've been taking classes on the side.
Congratulations on your pregnancy! That's exciting news. What is it about your current job that's making you want to make a change? Is it the work itself or the company culture?

I think it's a bit of both. I love the work, but the company culture can be pretty demanding. I've been working long hours for years and I'm starting to feel like I'm losing my work-life balance. I've also recently started painting and mushroom foraging, which has been a great way for me to relax and express myself.

It's great that you've found ways to relax and express yourself. Stand-up comedy can be a great way to challenge yourself and pursue your creative side. Have you thought about how you could balance your time between pursuing comedy and being a new parent?

Yeah, I've been thinking about that a lot. I'm planning to take a few months off after the baby is born to focus on being a new mom. After that, I'm thinking about scaling back my work hours and pursuing comedy more seriously. I've already started looking into comedy clubs and open mics in my area.

That sounds like a great plan. It's always good to have a support system in place, especially when you're starting a new family. Have you thought about how you'll make sure you have enough time for your comedy pursuits and your new role as a mom?

Yeah, I've been thinking about that a lot. I'm planning to hire a nanny to help with childcare, and my partner, Mike, is going to be very involved in taking care of the baby. I'm also thinking about joining a comedy group or finding a comedy mentor to help me get started.

It's great that you have a support system in place. Having a nanny and a supportive partner will definitely help. Joining a comedy group or finding a mentor can be a great way to get feedback and guidance as you start your comedy career.

I'm really excited about it. I've already started working on some new material and I'm planning to perform at an open mic night on May 15th. I'm a bit nervous, but I'm hoping it will be a great way to get my feet wet and see how I like it.

That's a great goal to have. Performing at an open mic night is a great way to get started and see how you like it. Just remember to have fun and don't be too hard on yourself. What kind of material are you planning to perform?

I'm planning to do a mix of observational comedy and storytelling. I've been drawing from my experiences as a marketing manager and my hobbies, like mushroom foraging and painting. I'm hoping to have a unique perspective and voice that will resonate with audiences.

That sounds like a great plan. Using your own experiences and observations can be a great way to create relatable and funny material. I think you have a good chance of success, especially with your creativity and enthusiasm.

Thanks, I'm feeling pretty positive about it. I'm just hoping that I can make it work and create a successful comedy career that I can be proud of. I'm planning to perform at least 10 times in the next 6 months and see how things go from there.

It's great that you have a clear vision for your comedy career. Performing regularly and getting feedback from audiences will be a great way to refine your material and grow as a comedian. Just remember to stay focused and keep working towards your goals.
I will, thanks for the advice. I'm also thinking about taking a course on home brewing beer and woodworking, just for fun. I love trying new things and learning new skills.

That's great! It's always good to have hobbies and interests outside of your main career. Home brewing beer and woodworking can be great ways to relax and express yourself creatively. Maybe you can even incorporate some of your hobbies into your comedy material.
Yeah, that's a great idea. I'll have to think about that. Thanks for chatting with me, it's been really helpful to talk through my thoughts and ideas.

You're welcome, it was my pleasure. I think you have a lot of potential as a comedian and I'm excited to see where your career takes you. Good luck with your upcoming performances and don't hesitate to reach out if you need any more advice or guidance.
